question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: latestFileContentsQuery (static)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GROUP BY f.entity_id in the outer query of latestFileContentsQuery combined with string_agg to force the database to buffer all matching file_data rows in a hash aggregate before returning a single result row, consuming memory proportional to the total size of all file_data blobs for the entity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getFileDataQuery (static)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nested correlated subquery structure of getFileDataQuery (outer query references inner subquery result as a range bound) to cause the database to execute the inner subquery multiple times or perform a nested loop scan, increasing CPU usage compared to a simple indexed lookup?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any request-level cost accounting in fallbackRetry() to issue a single API request that consumes the equivalent of 10 normal requests worth of database resources, effectively bypassing any per-request rate limiting that is based on request count rather than resource consumption?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a whereQuery that generates an innerWhere string containing the substring 'and ' multiple times, causing replaceAll('and ', 'and f.') to produce a WHERE clause with multiple table-alias prefixes that confuses the query planner or causes repeated condition evaluation, increasing query execution cost?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getFileData()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a fileId corresponding to a system file with a very long history of append operations and a timestamp of 0, causing getFileDataQuery to aggregate all historical file_data rows for that entity via string_agg, resulting in a response that requires the server to allocate memory proportional to the entire historical size of the file?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that each fallbackRetry() iteration appends a new consensus_timestamp < X condition to filters.whereQuery, causing the 10th iteration to execute a CTE query with 10 additional WHERE conditions, each requiring the database to evaluate an additional predicate on every scanned row, increasing per-row CPU cost?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests to the exchange rate endpoint, each triggering the full 10-iteration fallbackRetry() loop, causing the mirror node to hold multiple long-running database connections simultaneously and increasing total DB server CPU and memory usage by more than 30% compared to the preceding 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getLatestFileDataContents()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a whereQuery with a timestamp filter that is a very precise equality condition matching a known FileCreate timestamp, causing the latest_create CTE to return that specific timestamp and the outer query to aggregate all subsequent rows, potentially including a large number of FileAppend operations that were never intended to be aggregated in a single response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronous sequential nature of fallbackRetry()'s while loop (each await this.getLatestFileDataContents() blocks before the next iteration) to hold a Node.js async context and a database connection for the entire duration of 10 sequential queries, preventing those resources from being reused by other requests?
